all_cases.description: Template renders variables and static text.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('Hello {{ name }}!')
print(t.render(name='Alice'))
print(Template('plain').render())

all_cases.description: Dot notation resolves attributes, dictionary keys, and integer indexes.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
class Obj:
    x = 'attr'
t = Template('{{ obj.x }} {{ d.key }} {{ xs.1 }}')
print(t.render(obj=Obj(), d={'key': 'dict'}, xs=['zero', 'one']))

all_cases.description: Built-in filters transform a single value.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
print(Template('{{ name|upper }} {{ missing|default("guest") }} {{ items|join("-") }}').render(name='ada', items=['a', 'b']))

all_cases.description: If, elif, and else select the first true branch.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if a %}A{% elif b %}B{% else %}C{% endif %}')
print(t.render(a=0, b=1))
print(t.render(a=0, b=0))

all_cases.description: Comparisons and built-in tests work inside conditions.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if x > y %}bad{% elif n is odd %}odd{% else %}other{% endif %}')
print(t.render(x=1, y=2, n=3))
print(Template('{% if missing is undefined %}missing{% endif %}').render())

all_cases.description: For loops render items and else branches render for empty sequences.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% for item in items %}[{{ item }}]{% else %}empty{% endfor %}')
print(t.render(items=['a', 'b']))
print(t.render(items=[]))

all_cases.description: With blocks create scoped variable bindings.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ x }}|{% with x = "inner" %}{{ x }}{% endwith %}|{{ x }}')
print(t.render(x='outer'))

all_cases.description: A standalone block renders its body at the block location.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('before{% block main %}content{% endblock %}after')
print(t.render())

all_cases.description: Environment.from_string creates templates bound to environment globals.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment(globals={'site': 'Docs'})
t = env.from_string('{{ site }}:{{ page }}')
print(t.render(page='Intro'))

all_cases.description: Environment.get_template loads templates by name from a dictionary loader.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({'a.html': 'A={{ value }}'})
print(env.get_template('a.html').render(value=7))

all_cases.description: A child template overrides a named parent block.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({'base': 'H[{% block body %}base{% endblock %}]F', 'child': '{% extends "base" %}{% block body %}child{% endblock %}'})
print(env.get_template('child').render())

all_cases.description: Includes render named templates and imports expose macro namespaces.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({'part': 'P={{ x }}', 'mac': '{% macro wrap(v) %}[{{ v }}]{% endmacro %}'})
print(env.from_string('{% include "part" %}').render(x='one'))
print(env.from_string('{% import "mac" as m %}{{ m.wrap(x) }}').render(x='two'))

all_cases.description: Custom filters and tests are read from the environment registries.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment()
env.filters['bracket'] = lambda value: '[' + str(value) + ']'
env.tests['short'] = lambda value: len(value) < 4
t = env.from_string('{{ name|bracket }}:{% if name is short %}short{% else %}long{% endif %}')
print(t.render(name='Ada'))
print(t.render(name='Grace'))

all_cases.description: Undefined values render empty, are falsy, and can be tested.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('[{{ missing }}]{% if missing %}T{% else %}F{% endif %}:{% if missing is undefined %}U{% endif %}')
print(t.render())

all_cases.description: Autoescape escapes variables unless values are marked safe.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment(autoescape=True)
print(env.from_string('{{ x }}|{{ x|safe }}').render(x='<b>'))

all_cases.description: Whitespace trim markers trim adjacent spaces around expressions.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
print(Template('A {{- x -}} B').render(x='X'))
print(Template('A {% if ok -%} X {%- endif %} B').render(ok=True))

all_cases.description: Malformed control blocks raise TemplateSyntaxError at parse time.
Missing named templates raise TemplateNotFound.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment, TemplateNotFound
env = Environment({})
try:
    env.get_template('missing')
    print('no-error')
except TemplateNotFound:
    print('missing')

all_cases.description: Updating a loaded template invalidates the cache so direct loads and includes project the same new environment state.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({'item': 'v1 {{ site }}', 'page': '[{% include "item" %}]'}, globals={'site': 'Docs'})
print(env.get_template('page').render())
print(env.get_template('item').render())
env.set_template('item', 'v2 {{ site|upper }}')
print(env.get_template('page').render())
print(env.get_template('item').render())
all_cases.expected_output:
[v1 Docs]
v1 Docs
[v2 DOCS]
v2 DOCS
all_cases.actual:
[v1 Docs]
v1 Docs
[v2 DOCS]
v2 DOCS
all_cases.expected:
[v1 Docs]
v1 Docs
[v2 DOCS]
v2 DOCS

all_cases.description: Inherited blocks, parent includes, globals, and filters must all read from the same environment during render.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({
    'base': 'Title:{% block title %}{{ site }}{% endblock %}|{% include "nav" %}|Body:{% block body %}base{% endblock %}',
    'nav': 'Nav={{ site|lower }}',
    'child': '{% extends "base" %}{% block title %}{{ page|title }}{% endblock %}{% block body %}{% for item in items %}{{ item|upper }}{% endfor %}{% endblock %}',
}, globals={'site': 'DOCS'})
print(env.get_template('child').render(page='api guide', items=['a', 'b']))

all_cases.description: Cached templates must consult current filter and test registries at render time rather than freezing registry functions at compile time.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({'page': '{{ name|decorate }}:{% if name is selected %}Y{% else %}N{% endif %}'})
env.filters['decorate'] = lambda value: '<' + str(value) + '>'
env.tests['selected'] = lambda value: value.startswith('A')
t = env.get_template('page')
print(t.render(name='Ada'))
env.filters['decorate'] = lambda value: '[' + str(value).upper() + ']'
env.tests['selected'] = lambda value: value.endswith('e')
print(t.render(name='Grace'))
print(env.get_template('page').render(name='Bob'))

all_cases.description: Macro arguments, loop variables, includes, globals, and caller values passed as arguments remain correctly scoped across repeated renders.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({
    'macros': '{% macro row(item, p) %}{{ p }}:{{ item }}{% endmacro %}',
    'footer': '({{ item|default("none") }}|{{ prefix }})'
}, globals={'prefix': 'G'})
t = env.from_string('{% import "macros" as ui %}{% for item in items %}[{{ ui.row(item, prefix) }}]{% endfor %}{% include "footer" %}')
print(t.render(items=['a', 'b'], prefix='R'))
print(t.render(items=[], prefix='S'))

all_cases.description: Autoescape policy is shared by inherited blocks, includes, and imported macros while local safe/autoescape controls remain scoped.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment env = Environment({ 'base': '
{% block body %}{{ value }}{% endblock %}|{% include "tail" %}
', 'tail': '{{ value }}', 'mac': '{% macro show(x) %}{{ x }}{% endmacro %}', 'child': '{% extends "base" %}{% block body %}{% import "mac" as m %}{{ m.show(value) }}|{{ value|safe }}|{% autoescape false %}{{ value }}{% endautoescape %}{% endblock %}', }, autoescape=True) print(env.get_template('child').render(value=''))

all_cases.description: A failed template load/update must not corrupt cached templates, globals, or later inherited/include renders.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment, TemplateNotFound
env = Environment({'base': 'A{% block body %}{{ site }}{% endblock %}Z', 'child': '{% extends "base" %}{% block body %}{% include "part" %}{% endblock %}', 'part': 'P={{ site }}'}, globals={'site': 'Docs'})
print(env.get_template('child').render())
env.set_template('part', '{% include "missing" %}')
try:
    env.get_template('child').render()
    print('no-error')
except TemplateNotFound:
    print('missing')
env.set_template('part', 'Q={{ site|upper }}')
print(env.get_template('child').render())
print(env.get_template('part').render())

all_cases.description: Undefined handling is consistent through direct render, include, inherited block, filters, and tests.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({
    'base': '{% block body %}base{% endblock %}|{% include "probe" %}',
    'probe': '[{{ missing|default("fallback") }}:{% if missing is undefined %}U{% endif %}]',
    'child': '{% extends "base" %}{% block body %}{{ missing }}:{% if missing %}T{% else %}F{% endif %}{% endblock %}',
})
print(env.from_string('{{ missing|default("fallback") }}:{% if missing is undefined %}U{% endif %}').render())
print(env.get_template('child').render())

all_cases.description: Whitespace trim markers preserve their semantics across parent blocks, includes, and cache reuse after source replacement.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({
    'base': 'A {% block body %} B {% endblock %} C',
    'part': ' I {{- value -}} J ',
    'child': '{% extends "base" %}{% block body -%}{% include "part" %}{%- endblock %}',
})
print(env.get_template('child').render(value='X'))
env.set_template('part', ' K {{- value -}} L ')
print(env.get_template('child').render(value='Y'))
all_cases.expected_output:
A  IXJ  C
A  KYL  C
all_cases.actual:
A  IXJ  C
A  KYL  C
all_cases.expected:
A  IXJ  C
A  KYL  C

all_cases.description: Global and source mutations are observed consistently regardless of previous cache population order.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({'card': '{{ site }}:{{ title|upper }}', 'page': '<{% include "card" %}>'}, globals={'site': 'One'})
page = env.get_template('page')
print(page.render(title='alpha'))
env.globals['site'] = 'Two'
print(page.render(title='beta'))
env.set_template('card', '{{ site|lower }}={{ title|title }}')
print(page.render(title='gamma ray'))
print(env.get_template('card').render(title='delta'))

all_cases.description: Repeated renders of an inherited template depend only on the current context after nested loops and scoped assignments.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({'base': 'H{% block body %}base{% endblock %}F', 'child': '{% extends "base" %}{% block body %}{% for item in items %}{% with label = item %}[{{ label }}]{% endwith %}{% else %}{{ label|default("empty") }}{% endfor %}{% endblock %}'})
t = env.get_template('child')
print(t.render(items=['a', 'b'], label='outer'))
print(t.render(items=[], label='outer'))
print(t.render(items=['x']))

all_cases.description: Imported macros share caller environment filters, globals, and autoescape policy across calls.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment({'mac': '{% macro label(v) %}{{ prefix }}={{ v|decorate }}{% endmacro %}', 'page': '{% import "mac" as m %}{{ m.label(value) }}|{{ m.label(other) }}'}, autoescape=True, globals={'prefix': 'P'})
env.filters['decorate'] = lambda value: '<' + str(value) + '>'
print(env.get_template('page').render(value='a', other='<b>'))
env.filters['decorate'] = lambda value: '[' + str(value) + ']'
env.globals['prefix'] = 'Q'
print(env.get_template('page').render(value='c', other='<d>'))

all_cases.description: A missing include raises through the environment without corrupting cache entries or registry mutations used by later renders.
all_cases.test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment, TemplateNotFound
env = Environment({'page': 'A{% include "missing" %}Z', 'ok': '{{ value|mark }}'}, globals={'value': 'g'})
env.filters['mark'] = lambda value: '<' + str(value) + '>'
try:
    env.get_template('page').render()
    print('no-error')
except TemplateNotFound:
    print('missing')
print(env.get_template('ok').render(value='x'))
env.set_template('missing', 'M={{ value|mark }}')
print(env.get_template('page').render(value='y'))
